_comment: Per-assembly coverage floor enforced by tools/check-coverage.py in CI.
Values are minimums, not targets — see docs/spec/coverage-gate.md.
The gate only fails on a DROP below; bump them upward as new tests land.
Current ratchet (set 2026-06-27 after #126 §A/§B closed): every assembly
at 100% line. Branch floors are set ~1% below the measured value as a
noise guard against renderer / EF / MudBlazor minor-version drift. The
issue's original targets (Core/Skills/Api 100%, AI ≥99%, Web ≥95%,
Persistence baseline-or-better) are all met or exceeded.
Measured floors (CI, 2026-06-27 — these are the numbers that bind):
Persistence 100/94.4, Web 100/93.7
(AI is local-only; see note below. Local integration also showed
Persistence branch 97.2% — but CI's runs measure 94.4% because
different test projects exercise different branches of
FlowHubDbContext; the CI value is the gate-relevant one.)
FlowHub.AI is intentionally absent from the enforced thresholds today.
Coverlet on the GitHub-Actions runner doesn't instrument FlowHub.AI when
its tests live in FlowHub.Web.ComponentTests (transitive-dep tracking
issue we couldn't pin down in the gate-rollout window). Locally `just
test-coverage` reports AI at 100% — we're not losing coverage signal,
only enforcement. Restore the AI threshold once CI-vs-local parity is
fixed (follow-up issue).
Stack-order note: this PR lands AFTER the test PRs that lift each
assembly to its current floor (see #126's PR list). Landing it before
they merge would fail the gate on the next CI run.
